subject: LU-11830 lov: avoid signed vs. unsigned comparison

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Cannot build patch due to 14, messages:

  In file included from include/linux/kernfs.h:10:0,
                  from include/linux/sysfs.h:15,
                  from include/linux/kobject.h:21,
                  from /home/green/smt/git/lustre-release/lustre/include/obd_class.h:35,
                  from /home/green/smt/git/lustre-release/lustre/obdclass/lprocfs_status.c:39:
 /home/green/smt/git/lustre-release/lustre/obdclass/lprocfs_status.c: In function ‘short_io_bytes_store’:
 include/linux/kernel.h:784:17: error: comparison of distinct pointer types lacks a cast [-Werror]
   (void) (&_min1 == &_min2);  \
                  ^
 /home/green/smt/git/lustre-release/lustre/include/lustre_net.h:498:32: note: in expansion of macro ‘min’
  #define OBD_MAX_SHORT_IO_BYTES min((u32)max(PAGE_SIZE, 16UL * 1024UL), \
                                 ^
 /home/green/smt/git/lustre-release/lustre/obdclass/lprocfs_status.c:2382:48: note: in expansion of macro ‘OBD_MAX_SHORT_IO_BYTES’
   if (val && (val < MIN_SHORT_IO_BYTES || val > OBD_MAX_SHORT_IO_BYTES))
                                                 ^
 /home/green/smt/git/lustre-release/lustre/obdclass/lprocfs_status.c: At top level:
 cc1: error: unrecognized command line option "-Wno-format-truncation" [-Werror]

parents.subject: LU-8066 utils: have llapi_target_iterate use sysfs tree

commit.subject: LU-11830 lov: avoid signed vs. unsigned comparison
commit.message:
LU-11830 lov: avoid signed vs. unsigned comparison

In the expansion of do_div64() GCC complains about pointer comparison
because loff_t is not a u64 variable as it should be.  lov_do_div64()
also has signed vs. unsigned comparisons due to a signed loff_t.
Change lov_do_div() to use a 64-bit variable for do_div() instead of
loff_t to avoid these warnings.

Change OST_MAXREQSIZE and friends to be consistently unsigned values
to avoid compiler warnings.

Fix "lfs mirror resync" to avoid comparing signed and unsigned valued.

Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
